meta.title: The Atlas of Everything
meta.subtitle: a navigable matrix of proven mathematics & science, grown from 1 = 1

equations.name: Unity
equations.desc: The primordial axiom. All mathematics begins with identity: a thing equals itself.

equations.name: Duality
equations.desc: The first operation. Addition creates multiplicity from unity.

equations.name: Trinity
equations.desc: One number, three constructions: repeated addition, progression, multiplication.

equations.name: Quaternity
equations.desc: Square numbers appear; multiplication, powers and roots are introduced as one truth.

equations.name: Addition
equations.desc: Combine two quantities into a sum.

equations.name: Subtraction
equations.desc: The inverse of addition: remove one quantity from another.

equations.name: Multiplication
equations.desc: Repeated addition compressed into a single operation.

equations.name: Division
equations.desc: The inverse of multiplication: how many of b fit into a.

equations.name: Exponentiation
equations.desc: Repeated multiplication; the first genuinely nested operation.

equations.name: Roots
equations.desc: The inverse of a power; raising to a fractional exponent.

equations.name: Golden Ratio
equations.desc: The 'Life' constant: a length divided so the whole-to-large ratio equals large-to-small. Pervades growth and form.

equations.name: Pi
equations.desc: The ratio of any circle's circumference to its diameter.

equations.name: Euler's Number
equations.desc: The base of natural growth; the constant whose rate of change is itself.

equations.name: Imaginary Unit
equations.desc: The square root of minus one; opens the plane of complex numbers.

equations.name: Circumference
equations.desc: A circle's perimeter in terms of its radius.

equations.name: Area of a Circle
equations.desc: The area enclosed by a circle of radius r.

equations.name: Pythagorean Theorem
equations.desc: In a right triangle, the squares on the legs sum to the square on the hypotenuse.

equations.name: Volume of a Sphere
equations.desc: The volume enclosed by a sphere; integral of circular cross-sections.

equations.name: Distance Formula
equations.desc: The straight-line distance between two points; Pythagoras in coordinates.

equations.name: Linear Equation
equations.desc: A straight line: constant slope m and intercept b.

equations.name: Quadratic Formula
equations.desc: The roots of any second-degree polynomial in closed form.

equations.name: Binomial Theorem
equations.desc: Expands a power of a sum into a weighted sum of products.

equations.name: Logarithm
equations.desc: The inverse of exponentiation: the power to which a base must be raised.

equations.name: Exponential Growth
equations.desc: Each step multiplies the previous value; the file's J-curve from a tiny seed.

equations.name: Logarithmic Growth
equations.desc: Fast rise then flattening; the file's diminishing-returns curve.

equations.name: Logistic Growth
equations.desc: The middle ground: exponential early, saturating at a ceiling K.

equations.name: Pythagorean Identity
equations.desc: The unit circle written as an identity between sine and cosine.

equations.name: Law of Cosines
equations.desc: Generalises Pythagoras to any triangle.

equations.name: Fourier Series
equations.plain: f = a0/2 + sum(an cos nx + bn sin nx)
equations.desc: Any periodic signal is a sum of sines and cosines.

equations.name: Limit
equations.desc: The value a function approaches; the foundation of calculus.

equations.name: Derivative
equations.desc: The instantaneous rate of change of a function.

equations.name: Integral
equations.desc: The accumulated area under a curve; a limit of sums.

equations.name: Fundamental Theorem of Calculus
equations.desc: Differentiation and integration are inverse operations.

equations.name: Taylor Series
equations.desc: Approximate any smooth function as an infinite polynomial.

equations.name: Series for e^x
equations.desc: The exponential function as its own Taylor series; it equals its own derivative.

equations.name: Matrix Multiplication
equations.desc: Composition of linear transformations as a sum of products.

equations.name: Determinant (2x2)
equations.desc: The signed area/volume scaling factor of a linear map.

equations.name: Eigenvalue Equation
equations.desc: Directions a transformation only stretches; the backbone of quantum mechanics and PCA.

equations.name: Dot Product
equations.desc: Projects one vector onto another; links algebra to angle.

equations.name: Euler's Formula
equations.plain: e^(i t) = cos t + i sin t
equations.desc: Rotation in the complex plane; ties exponential growth to circular motion.

equations.name: Euler's Identity
equations.desc: The 'mystery' equation: e, i, pi, 1 and 0 in one line.

equations.name: Cauchy Integral Formula
equations.plain: f(a)=1/(2 pi i) oint f(z)/(z-a) dz
equations.desc: A holomorphic function's value is fixed by its boundary.

equations.name: Prime Number Theorem
equations.desc: Primes thin out like x over its natural logarithm.

equations.name: Fibonacci & Golden Ratio
equations.desc: Each term is the sum of the previous two; ratios converge to phi.

equations.name: Riemann Zeta Function
equations.desc: Encodes the distribution of primes; its zeros are mathematics' deepest open question.

equations.name: Normal Distribution
equations.desc: The bell curve: sums of many independent effects converge to it.

equations.name: Bayes' Theorem
equations.desc: How evidence updates belief; the engine of inference and machine learning.

equations.name: Expected Value
equations.desc: The long-run average of a random quantity.

equations.name: Shannon Entropy
equations.desc: The average information content of a message; the limit of compression.

equations.name: Channel Capacity
equations.desc: The maximum error-free data rate of a noisy channel.

equations.name: Newton's Second Law
equations.desc: Force equals mass times acceleration; the law of motion.

equations.name: Newton's Gravitation
equations.desc: Every mass attracts every other, falling off with the square of distance.

equations.name: Kinetic Energy
equations.desc: The energy of motion; quadratic in speed.

equations.name: Euler-Lagrange Equation
equations.desc: Nature follows the path of stationary action; reframes all of mechanics.

equations.name: Hamilton's Equations
equations.latex: \dot q=\frac{\partial H}{\partial p},\;\dot p=-\frac{\partial H}{\partial q}
equations.desc: Energy as the generator of motion; the bridge to quantum mechanics.

equations.name: Coulomb's Law
equations.plain: F = 1/(4 pi eps0) q1 q2 / r^2
equations.desc: The electric force between charges; same inverse-square form as gravity.

equations.name: Maxwell's Equations
equations.plain: div E = rho/eps0 ; curl B = mu0 J + mu0 eps0 dE/dt
equations.desc: Four equations unifying electricity, magnetism and light.

equations.name: Speed of Light
equations.desc: Light's speed emerges from the constants of electromagnetism alone.

equations.name: Ideal Gas Law
equations.desc: Pressure, volume and temperature of an ideal gas are linked by one relation.

equations.name: Boltzmann Entropy
equations.desc: Entropy counts the microstates of a system; links the unseen to the measurable.

equations.name: First Law of Thermodynamics
equations.desc: Energy is conserved: internal energy changes by heat in minus work out.

equations.name: Mass-Energy Equivalence
equations.desc: Mass is concentrated energy; the most famous equation in physics.

equations.name: Lorentz Factor
equations.desc: Time and length distort as speeds approach light; the heart of special relativity.

equations.name: Einstein Field Equations
equations.plain: G_uv + L g_uv = 8 pi G / c^4 T_uv
equations.desc: Matter tells spacetime how to curve; spacetime tells matter how to move.

equations.name: Planck Relation
equations.desc: Energy comes in discrete packets proportional to frequency.

equations.name: de Broglie Wavelength
equations.desc: Every particle is also a wave, with wavelength set by its momentum.

equations.name: Schrodinger Equation
equations.desc: The wavefunction's evolution; the master equation of the quantum world.

equations.name: Heisenberg Uncertainty
equations.desc: Position and momentum cannot both be sharp; a hard limit on knowledge.

equations.name: Arrhenius Equation
equations.desc: Reaction rate rises exponentially with temperature.

equations.name: Nernst Equation
equations.plain: E = E0 - (R T/n F) ln Q
equations.desc: The voltage of an electrochemical cell from concentrations.

equations.name: Gibbs Free Energy
equations.desc: Predicts whether a reaction proceeds; balances energy against entropy.

equations.name: Hardy-Weinberg Principle
equations.desc: Allele frequencies stay constant across generations without selection.

equations.name: Michaelis-Menten Kinetics
equations.desc: Enzyme reaction rate saturates as substrate increases.

equations.name: Logistic Population Model
equations.desc: Population grows fast then levels at the carrying capacity K.

equations.name: Compound Interest
equations.desc: Money grows exponentially as interest earns interest.

equations.name: Continuous Compounding
equations.desc: The limit of compounding infinitely often; pure exponential growth.

equations.name: Black-Scholes Equation
equations.plain: dV/dt + 1/2 s^2 S^2 d2V/dS2 + r S dV/dS - rV = 0
equations.desc: Prices an option by treating risk as diffusion; born from the heat equation.
